name: Echo River Crossing
descriptionSeed: The old stone-and-cable bridge where the Echo River narrows between granite shoulders. Upstream the water sheets gray-green over the rocks — the contamination is unmistakable here, worse every week. Fisher shacks stand empty. The bridge is the only crossing for miles, so everyone passes through: Harmonic traders, Radiant surveyors, valley farmers, and lately, strangers no one recognizes. The old bridge-stone at the crossing is a waygate, and the least ceremonious one in the world — carters queue behind it, and the ferrymen charge the same either way.
encounterFlavor: Travelers with rumors, contamination survey teams from both Heights and Plateau blaming each other, desperate fishers, and things washed downstream that shouldn't exist.
questSeeds: Both civilizations' survey teams are collecting samples and hiding results from each other; getting honest data means winning one side's trust — or stealing both
The empty fisher shacks aren't all empty: someone upstream-sick is hiding there, afraid of being quarantined away from family
A trader's barge snagged something in the shallows that isn't stone and isn't wood, and the crew won't touch it twice
Toll-takers who never used to be here now charge crossing fees in Radiant scrip — someone is testing how much authority a uniform buys

encounterSeeds.hint: The granite shoulder above the path has been shedding — a slide could cut the crossing

axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
worldPosNote: Near-centre folk country — the Valley sits where all axes cross faintly, so longitude spreads and colatitude stays low. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
